message:
fix: add transitive parent-blocked check to GetActionableIssues (bv-108)

GetActionableIssues() previously only checked direct blocks-type
dependencies when determining if an issue was actionable. Parent-child
relationships were treated as entirely non-blocking. This diverged from
br's (beads_rust) behavior, which enforces parent-blocked transitivity:
if a parent issue is blocked by something, all its children should also
be considered blocked.

The fix adds a three-phase approach:
1. Compute directly blocked issues (existing blocks-type logic)
2. Build a parent→children index from parent-child dependencies
3. BFS-propagate blocked status through parent-child edges (depth-capped
   at 50, matching br's MAX_DEPTH)

Importantly, parent-child alone still does NOT block — a child with an
open, unblocked parent remains actionable. Only when the parent is
itself blocked (by blocks-type deps or transitively) do children inherit
the blocked state.

Added 6 new test cases covering:
- Direct parent-blocked transitivity
- Deep (grandparent) transitive chains
- Parent not blocked → child still actionable
- Parent closed → child actionable
- Blocker resolved → parent and child both unblocked
- Multiple children all blocked when parent is blocked

Closes #108

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
